bead.title: federation: forward documentWrite to project owner
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Hub-mode document saves for spoke-owned projects have no owner-targeted documentWrite forwarding path, so a hub can only resolve local behavior instead of persisting the edit on the owning spoke.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_docs.go:34 defines DocumentWrite as a local path/content mutation with no project-owner forwarding semantics.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_docs.go:55 writes content directly with os.WriteFile, so hub-mode requests are not routed to the owning project.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:3263 exposes documentWrite(path, content) without owner/forwarding metadata or expected version input needed for safe federation.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-021-dashboard-ui.md:356 requires spec/document edits to be project-scoped, path-confined, and owner-targeted in hub-mode.
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:353 defines TC-019 federated document write coverage.

PROPOSED FIX
- Route hub-to-spoke documentWrite mutations through owner-targeted forwarding when the target project is owned by a spoke.
- Include origin/forwarding metadata so the spoke can distinguish forwarded hub requests from direct local writes and avoid loops.
- Preserve the same path confinement and expected-hash conflict semantics on the spoke-side write path.
- Ensure the forwarded write persists on the spoke and does not create or overwrite a hub-local file for the spoke project.

NON-SCOPE
Do not build a full collaborative editor or arbitrary filesystem browser. Keep the primary workflow scoped to project documents, especially docs/helix/**. Do not redesign the federation protocol beyond the minimum documentWrite forwarding metadata. Do not implement frontend Playwright coverage in this bead. Parent bead: ddx-d5dfa702.
bead.acceptance:
1. Go test TestFederatedDocumentWrite_ForwardsToOwner proves hub saves for spoke projects persist on the spoke and not locally.
2. Go test TestFederatedDocumentWrite_PreservesExpectedHashConflict proves stale expected-hash failures are returned from the owning spoke without overwriting spoke content.
3. Go test TestFederatedDocumentWrite_RejectsForwardingLoop proves origin/forwarding metadata prevents recursive hub-spoke forwarding.
4. cd cli && go test -short ./internal/server/graphql/... ./internal/federation/... passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
